predictions.title: Hyperscaler-AI-lab capital coupling" graduates from finance pact to compute-stack lock-in by Q3 2026.
predictions.body: **"Hyperscaler-AI-lab capital coupling" graduates from finance pact to compute-stack lock-in by Q3 2026.** Apr 24's Google **$40B + 5GW** to Anthropic + the prior Amazon **$25B + 5GW Trainium2/3** + Microsoft's existing $13B+ to OpenAI mean the **top 3 frontier labs each have a hyperscaler underwriter — paid partly in compute capacity, not cash**. By Q3 2026, **frontier-model serving topology becomes dictated by funding origin**: Anthropic Claude Opus 4.7 / 4.8 ships natively on Trainium2 + TPU v8 (Amazon + Google paths), OpenAI GPT-5.5 / 6 stays Azure-first, and Chinese frontier labs (DeepSeek V4 / Qwen 3.6-3.7) double down on Huawei Ascend (per the Apr 26 Bloomberg-CCTV piece). The "model-portability promise" of 2024-2025 quietly ends; **enterprise multi-cloud LLM strategy shifts from "multi-runtime" to "multi-frontier-vendor."** In plain language: In plain language: In plain language:

reasoning.because: Google's Apr 24 $40B + 5GW to Anthropic stacks on Amazon's $25B + 5GW Trainium2/3 and Microsoft's $13B+ to OpenAI.
reasoning.given: Each top-3 frontier lab now has a hyperscaler underwriter paid largely in dedicated compute capacity rather than cash.
reasoning.so_that: Frontier-model serving topology aligns with funding origin and the 2024-2025 model-portability promise quietly ends.
reasoning.landing: By Q3 2026; enterprise multi-cloud strategy shifts from multi-runtime to multi-frontier-vendor.
reasoning.plain_language: Big AI labs now get paid in cloud capacity, so each model runs best on its sponsor's chips. Companies must pick vendors, not just runtimes.

predictions.summary: Google's Apr 24 $40B + 5GW commitment to Anthropic, Amazon's $25B + 5GW Trainium2/3 deal, and Microsoft's $13B+ to OpenAI mean each top-3 frontier lab has a hyperscaler underwriter paid in compute. By Q3 2026, model serving topology is expected to track funding origin and enterprise multi-cloud strategy is expected to shift from multi-runtime to multi-frontier-vendor.

predictions.title: Inference servers become the new "primitive supply chain"
predictions.body: **Inference servers become the new "primitive supply chain" — Q3 2026 sees the first AI Infra-specific CVE class (LLM-INFRA-CWE) with mandatory NVD tagging.** The Apr 19-26 sequence — **LMDeploy CVE-2026-33626 SSRF (12h31m exploited)** + **SGLang CVE-2026-5760 (CVSS 9.8) Jinja2 SSTI RCE** + **Pillar's Antigravity sandbox-escape via `find_by_name -X`** + the Apr 21 Comment-and-Control prompt-injection chain across Claude Code / Gemini CLI / Copilot — composes a clear "inference-runtime + agentic-CI" attack-surface category. By Q3 2026, NIST/NVD adds a dedicated **"AI-Infra"** tag to its CVE schema, OWASP's LLM Top 10 v2026 promotes **"Inference Server Template / Loader RCE"** to a top-3 entry, and supply-chain SBOM tooling (Snyk / Wiz / Pillar / Lasso / Lakera) ships **per-engine signature feeds for vLLM / SGLang / LMDeploy / Triton / Ollama** as standard SKUs. In plain language: In plain language: In plain language:

reasoning.because: LMDeploy SSRF (12h31m exploited), SGLang CVE-2026-5760 (CVSS 9.8 Jinja2 SSTI RCE), Antigravity find_by_name sandbox escape, and Comment-and-Control chain land in one window.
reasoning.given: Inference runtimes plus agentic-CI now form a coherent attack-surface category that legacy SBOM tooling cannot map onto existing CWE schema.
reasoning.so_that: NIST/NVD adds an AI-Infra tag, OWASP LLM Top 10 promotes Inference Server Template / Loader RCE to top-3, and SBOM vendors ship per-engine signature feeds.
reasoning.landing: By Q3 2026; vLLM / SGLang / LMDeploy / Triton / Ollama get per-engine signature SKUs from Snyk / Wiz / Pillar / Lasso / Lakera.
reasoning.plain_language: AI servers got hacked four different ways in a week. Soon the official vulnerability catalog will get a new section just for AI infrastructure bugs.

predictions.summary: The Apr 19-26 LMDeploy SSRF, SGLang CVE-2026-5760 Jinja2 SSTI RCE, Antigravity sandbox escape, and Comment-and-Control prompt-injection chain compose an inference-runtime plus agentic-CI attack-surface category. By Q3 2026, NIST/NVD is expected to add an AI-Infra CVE tag, OWASP LLM Top 10 is expected to promote Inference Server Template RCE to top-3, and SBOM vendors are expected to ship per-engine signature feeds.

predictions.title: The "consumer-GPU coding agent" displaces the cloud-only coding API for ≥30% of dev workloads by Q3 2026.
predictions.body: **The "consumer-GPU coding agent" displaces the cloud-only coding API for ≥30% of dev workloads by Q3 2026.** Apr 22's Qwen3.6-27B (dense, beats 397B-A17B on SWE-bench Verified, **40 tok/s on RTX 3090 at Q4_K_M ~16.8 GB**) + Apr 23 Ollama MLX preview (1.57x prefill / 1.93x decode on M5 Max for Qwen3.5-35B-A3B) + DeepSeek V4-Pro 75%-off API (**effectively ~28x cheaper than Claude Opus 4.7**) compose a **3-corner squeeze** on the proprietary frontier-API SaaS model. By Q3 2026, **enterprise dev tools default to a hybrid harness** — local Qwen 3.6-class for "coding, refactor, lint, test" + remote frontier API only for "long-horizon planning / multi-tool reasoning" — and all Big-3 IDEs (Cursor / Windsurf / Kiro Power) ship a **local-first toggle** by default. "Cloud-only coding agent" becomes a 2024-era artifact. In plain language: In plain language: In plain language:

reasoning.because: Qwen3.6-27B beats 397B-A17B on SWE-bench Verified at 40 tok/s on RTX 3090, Ollama MLX hits 1.57x / 1.93x on M5 Max, and DeepSeek V4-Pro is ~28x cheaper than Opus 4.7.
reasoning.given: Quality, throughput, and price all clear the proprietary-frontier-API bar at consumer-GPU and Apple-Silicon scale in the same window.
reasoning.so_that: Enterprise dev tools default to a hybrid harness running local for coding / refactor / lint / test and remote only for long-horizon planning.
reasoning.landing: By Q3 2026; Cursor / Windsurf / Kiro Power ship a local-first toggle as default and cloud-only coding becomes a 2024-era artifact.
reasoning.plain_language: A laptop GPU can now run a coding model that beats the giant cloud ones. So most coding work will move off the cloud bill in about a year.

predictions.summary: Apr 22's Qwen3.6-27B beating 397B-A17B on SWE-bench Verified at 40 tok/s on RTX 3090, Apr 23's Ollama MLX preview hitting 1.57x prefill / 1.93x decode on M5 Max, and DeepSeek V4-Pro at ~28x cheaper than Opus 4.7 squeeze the cloud-only coding API. By Q3 2026, enterprise dev tools are expected to default to a hybrid harness and Cursor / Windsurf / Kiro Power are expected to ship local-first toggles.
